sector.name: Gold Finance
sector.description: Gold loan NBFCs benefiting from secured credit demand

fyPerformance: FY26 AUM stood at ₹36,623 Cr (+60% YoY); FY26 NII stood at ₹1,998 Cr (+50% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹949 Cr (+98% YoY)
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹18,145 Cr (+116% YoY); Q4FY26 NII stood at ₹596 Cr (+56% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹283 Cr (+59% YoY)
FY26 ROA stood at 3.5% and ROE stood at 16.5%; Gross Stage 3 improved to 0.9%, while Net Stage 3 stood at 0.5%
highlights: Gold loan AUM stood at ₹16,965 Cr, up 111% YoY and 33% QoQ
Gold loan mix stood around 46% of AUM and management expects it to move closer to 50%
Branch network stood at 1,429 branches after adding 318 branches in FY26
Company added 98 branches in Q4FY26, including 89 gold loan branches
Gold loan branch productivity improved from ₹14 Cr to ₹17 Cr per branch; 90% of gold loan branches are above breakeven AUM of ₹5 Cr per branch
Gold loan GNPA stood at 0.3%, among the best in the industry
Co-lending AUM stood at ₹7,783 Cr, up 91% YoY and around 21% of total AUM
Capri Global Capital Ltd.quote: "Capri is outperforming the sector on AUM growth, PAT growth, gold loan scale-up and operating leverage."

fyPerformance: FY26 AUM stood at ₹20,153 Cr (+28% YoY); AUM excluding business loans grew 41% YoY
FY26 disbursement stood at ₹31,410 Cr (+67% YoY); Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹11,665 Cr (+109% YoY)
FY26 NII stood at ₹1,230 Cr (+15% YoY); Q4FY26 NII stood at ₹349 Cr (+23% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹344 Cr (+53% YoY); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹101 Cr (+40% YoY)
FY26 ROA stood at 2.4% and ROE stood at 12.6%; Q4FY26 ROA stood at 2.6% and ROE stood at 14%

guidance.value: Gold loans and LAP twin-engine strategy

highlights: Gold loan AUM crossed ₹10,000 Cr and stood at ₹10,352 Cr, up 76% YoY
Gold loans now contribute around 51% of total AUM; gold tonnage grew 12% YoY to 12.6 tons
Mortgage AUM stood at ₹9,362 Cr, up 16% YoY; doorstep gold loan AUM stood at ₹1,730 Cr, up 108% YoY
Branch network stood at 757 branches across 17 states and UTs after adding 148 branches in FY26
Company assigned and derecognized ₹886 Cr business loan portfolio, reducing unsecured exposure
Secured on-book ratio now stands above 99.5%; Gross Stage 3 stood at 1.9%, while Net Stage 3 stood at 1.3%
Fedbank Financial Services Ltd.quote: "Fedbank is outperforming the sector on gold loan growth, secured portfolio shift and PAT growth, but LAP execution remains a key monitorable."

fyPerformance: Loan AUM stood at ₹1,08,180 Cr (+38% YoY and +10% QoQ)
FY26 total income stood at ₹7,626 Cr (+38% YoY); Q4FY26 total income stood at ₹2,090 Cr (+51% YoY)
FY26 PAT pre-NCI stood at ₹1,817 Cr (+214% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT pre-NCI stood at ₹623 Cr (+148% YoY and +24% QoQ)
Q4FY26 PPOP stood at ₹1,173 Cr (+80% YoY); Q4FY26 ROA stood at 3% and ROE stood at 18%
highlights: Management said the company is now anchored on secured lending, co-lending/off-book growth and an AI-led operating model
Gold loan AUM stood at ₹52,581 Cr, up 150% YoY and 21% QoQ
Gold loan book forms around 91% of standalone AUM; gold tonnage stood around 60 MT and LTV stood at 63%
Core product AUM stood at ₹1,04,198 Cr and formed around 96% of total AUM
Assigned loan book stood at ₹23,704 Cr, up 85% YoY; co-lending book stood at ₹14,384 Cr, up 36% YoY
Gross NPA stood at 1.5%, Net NPA stood at 0.7% and provision coverage stood at 93%
IIFL Finance Ltd.quote: "IIFL is outperforming the sector on gold loan rebound, AUM growth, PAT recovery and co-lending scale-up."

fyPerformance: Consolidated AUM stood at ₹63,798 Cr (+48% YoY); standalone AUM stood at ₹55,952 Cr (+69% YoY)
Consolidated revenue from operations stood at ₹9,509 Cr; Q4FY26 revenue from operations stood at ₹2,614 Cr (+11% YoY and +11% QoQ)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹405 Cr vs loss in Q4FY25; FY26 PAT stood at ₹993 Cr
Q4FY26 ROA stood at 2.8% and ROE stood at 12.3%; FY26 ROA stood at 2% and ROE stood at 7.7%
Standalone PAT stood at ₹1,525 Cr; capital adequacy stood at 21.3%

guidance.value: Expected to be better than FY26
Gradual improvement in asset quality and collections

highlights: Gold loan remained the core growth driver in Q4FY26
Consolidated gold loan AUM stood at ₹50,953 Cr, up 99% YoY and 32% QoQ
Gold loans formed 80% of consolidated AUM vs 59% last year; standalone gold loan AUM stood at ₹48,814 Cr, up 98% YoY
Gold loan LTV remained stable at 57%; gold tonnage stood at around 63 MT
New gold loan regulations allow the company to launch consumption loans and income-generating loans
Microfinance remains in recovery phase, but management expects gradual improvement in asset quality and collections
Manappuram Finance Ltd.quote: "Manappuram is outperforming the sector on gold loan AUM growth, but consolidated profitability is still impacted by MFI and non-gold stress."

fyPerformance: Consolidated AUM stood at ₹1,81,916 Cr (+49% YoY)
Consolidated gold loan AUM stood at ₹1,65,000 Cr (+54% YoY)
Standalone loan book stood at ₹1,64,000 Cr
Standalone PAT stood at ₹10,134 Cr (+95% YoY); consolidated PAT stood at ₹10,607 Cr (+98% YoY)
Dividend declared stood at ₹30 per share; group opened 177 branches during FY26
highlights: Management highlighted that FY26 saw the highest-ever consolidated gold loan AUM
Consolidated gold loan AUM increased by ₹57,000 Cr during FY26
Muthoot remains the largest gold loan franchise in India
India still has around 25,000-30,000 tons of household gold, giving long-term growth runway for gold loans
Muthoot Money gold loan AUM stood at ₹9,794 Cr, up 151% YoY; customer base increased from 2.74 lakh to 4.6 lakh
Average gold loan LTV stood around 57%
Muthoot Finance Ltd.quote: "Muthoot is outperforming the sector on scale, profitability, balance sheet strength and gold loan franchise depth."
